Nice.

We can get started.
think I want two things that you have.

And it's...

And then, you know, when you provide feedback, Marisol, who did these pages, is going to see it and she can implement it.
Yeah, got it.

Got it.

Um, so yeah, it's going to be very, very similar across the board.

Got it.
And for the part of it that has code in it, right?
There's some attack vector and certain sections that have a kind of either a configuration line or a code in it.
So that is also AI generated and so do we validate it or it's just fine to keep it as?
So this is AI generated and it includes like a disclaimer right here that says it's pseudocode representation of how unauthorized access might be simulated.
I think this is going to be reviewed by someone from your security team, right?
Yeah.
So I think, yeah, I mean, from our experience, the AI generated code is accurate and it's, you know, it's, it doesn't like, it's not incorrect.
Like usually it's, it accurately.

Picks like the issue, but I think if we find that, like, if they find any issues, let us know, but we haven't had, like, I don't think, I can't recall with any of my clients who were, had code snippets that the code snippets were incorrect.

At one point, we had, we were creating CVE pages for a different client, and we actually had a developer come in and just review all of the code, and they said, this is correct, like, nothing is here, it's, like, off, so, yeah, but, you know, feel free to take a look and let me know what you think.

All right, yeah, I mean, I'm just trying to be a bit proactive here, so that some things are, like, taken care of, all right, and, cool, I think, I'll look at these pages, also pass this internally to Mahati, so, idea.
She'd also be sharing these pages to someone in, say, security teams, who would kind of like to look, have a look at the structure, content, and how the pages would be put up.
All right.
I'll have these shared.
I hope you'll have these links to me right after this.
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
I'm going to drop it in chat, but I'm going to send them also in Slack, so that Mahadi can see it as well.
Yeah.
And you all should have access, like the people, you, Mahadi, and I think you might also have editing permissions to add other people.
Yeah, I think I've been adding some folks from my team to just have some comments in.
Yeah, and there would be comments from other team members.

Like, say, from Christian and Patrick on the design elements and things that have been used, and just wanted to make sure that those are taken care of, like, not for the samples, but for when we get to the development phase.
Yeah, yeah, yeah, okay.
Perfect.
I think there are comments from our end on the design pages, and then, I mean, your team can take it up from here for design and then the next steps.
Yeah, yeah, yeah, of course.
Okay, we have that meeting later today with Nicholas and Jory.
I'm going to be there as well.

And to Jory, as well, if needed.

I think it's, you know, just to iron out, I think we talked about this last week, but it's just Nicholas needs to, like, understand, get access to the CMS so he knows how to map everything correctly from our workflow to your CMS.
So those are, and I know you have, like, you know, strong access controls to your CMS, so it's just about ironing that out.
Yeah, absolutely.

So glad that you raised it last week.

So I think that was the gap.

And then it's like, okay.
Okay.
Let's make this happen.
And I'm sure accesses and other things can just be unlocked.
So it's also important for our web team to kind of know, you know, what integration help we need to offer and what bandwidth we need to free up for this.
So I think that's the larger idea.
It's going to be pretty late for me, but if I'll be up, I'll probably try and join the call because this project's important for me.
I won't really want this to meet timelines.
So, yeah, I mean, I mean, just like you, I'll be more like I'll not be speaking much, but I'll just be consuming and making sure things are aligned.
Yeah, yeah, for sure.
No worries.
mean, even if you don't make it, it's totally fine.
I think I have enough context from you and just from our meeting so far to kind of help everyone get on the same page.
But by the way, on the dev side, like.
So I know for a fact that workflow is in the making and it's ready on the publishing side or integrating it with content stack as our CMS.
So have thoughts been given by your web team already or we'll explore once you get accesses?
So at what stage are we currently in?
So you are using your CMS.
I thought it was sanity.
Content stack.
Oh, it's content stack.
Okay, don't worry.
Yeah, no worries.

So when it comes to like right now, this is like as soon as we get access to the CMS, they can immediately start like understanding how the CMS works, like what are the different, what's like the structure, if that's what you're asking.
So it can move pretty quickly from here.
It just depends depends on.
Like how the CMS is set up, like what are the, what's the template, and things like that.
Understood.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Okay.
Okay.
I don't know, for some reason, I thought you were on Sanity, but you're on ContentStack.
Okay.
No problem.
Well, yeah, I just let Nicholas know about ContentStack and that that's our CMS.
And maybe, you know, when we kind of meet, then things can come over.
Yeah.
Yeah, yeah.
Yeah, will let him know.
I think ContentStack, it's a headless CMS, right?
As far as I know.
Yeah, it's a headless CMS.
Yeah, I think it should be, I don't, I don't anticipate there to be like any big issues.
The one CMS that has been problematic in the past was Prismic.
So if you were using Prismic, I would kind of be a little bit concerned because I've heard things that's just the way Prismic is said.
said.
It's incredibly complicated to set up programmatic publishing, like programmatic content publishing, but I'll let Nicholas know for sure.
Okay, nice.
Aside from that, so for the AI content update, so we, Marisol is working on the two blogs for this week.
We're going to have them ready for you by the end of this week, and Sydney has also implemented edits on the first blog that we delivered for you.
So if you want, you can stage and publish it.
You can take a look at the second version.
It's a lot longer.
I think it's over 3,000 words long.
So you can let her know if it's like, you know, if it's hitting the marks on what you want for CS 101 content.
All right.
I think that Mansi can take up this next step, and she can take care of it.
Okay.
Okay.
Perfect.
All right.
Anything else?
Oh, question.
Where do we get the data for keyword volumes in the Airtable?
So quick sort of to zoom back a little bit.
So we took the approved topics from here.
And then we just adjusted the data here so that it's the same.
And the keyword search volume comes from SEMrush.
So it's just the SEMrush export.
Sometimes it depends on like if the way we generate assignments, it can be done in two ways.
So we can use a keyword or we can use a URL.
So let's say like.
If you find a blog post by one of your competitors and you really want to publish something similar, we can use that URL, plug it into our workflows, and then it's going to generate an assignment.
And in that case, the search volume is going to contain the search volume for all of the keywords that that blog post ranks for.
So it's not just one specific keyword, it's all of it, really.

So sometimes you might be seeing for prompt hacking, well, prompt hacking is very top of the funnel, but there are some keywords that are just very high volumes, and that's just because it's not a single keyword, it's all of the keywords that the URL ranks for.

If we just use a single keyword, then it's just going to be reflective of that one keyword, like AI penetration testing, but this data and the keyword difficulty data comes from SEMrush, so that's what we use.
Got it, so like for the topic behavioral, and what was that topic?

AI cybersecurity or AI detection, so behavioral AI versus traditional security was the term, which has row number 12, which has 3600 volumes, so someone from my team is discussing this internally, someone from the team reported that this number kind of mismatches, but I take the reasoning now, it's not just for the direct term, but more around the topic, and that's how you call it.

Yeah, yeah, yeah, it's not, yeah, it's not just a single keyword, so you're not the first one to ask that, we had another client who was in the healthcare space, and they were seeing like search volumes that were quite high, and were like, how is this, it's like, well, it's not just one, it's multiple, but I'll pass this along to Sydney, maybe just for, maybe it's just a little bit too confusing, we might like switch to just using like search volume of a single keyword, maybe that's simpler.
Simpler.

Yeah, so internally with SEO, have been following pillar and cluster strategy and kind of identifying a topic as a pillar when it's a parent topic and then a topic, supporting topics would become clusters.
So that's how we have been structuring content and, you know, all these skips and yeses are kind of influenced from some of that analysis, the strategy that we have internally.
And then I saw your questions around some skips of why we would, so I think this AI, I think I made a note.
Yeah, three ones were, yeah, these are the three.
Yeah, these are the three.
So listicles in general, we were not, so we are not writing since the last quarter.
So you'd see a lot of listicle articles on our cybersecurity 101, but we have.
Not written more because there was a discussion within brand where we want to give screen time and then visibility to competitors on a content like that.
So there's been some communication around that that's ongoing.
That's the reason we want to snooze this for now and push this maybe for next month or so.

But yeah, this is for listicles and the other one, I think again from SEO analysis, let's just target AI versus traditional security as a primary keyword because the SEO person kind of got confused between behavioral AI versus traditional security being the primary keyword.
So that's the reason, but yeah, that that was a kind of a miss so we can just target and
So, yes, for that topic.
Okay.
Okay.
Sounds good.
Yeah, I do remember you talking about the listicles.
Yeah, it's totally fine if you want to put it on the back burner for now.

think, like, yeah, I think now with, like, LLMs and AI, I think, like, marketing teams can kind of use that in discussions and basically being like, well, a lot of this content is, like, going to be read by LLMs.

So this is, like, a unique, I mean, it's a good opportunity to just kind of convince people using AI that you want to, you know, it's, like, a good argument because we do see, like, you know, greater visibility from these types of assignments, even though they're kind of, you know, they're very, they're, you know, pure SEO plays.
And I totally understand that, like, a lot of companies, they don't want to talk about their competitors and especially big.

Enterprises, because sometimes, I mean, I've written content for really big companies where they were against mentioning their competitors because they were afraid of, like, any potential legal implications.

And in the cases that they allowed us to write about competitors, it was only, like, we could never talk about their, like, cons or, like, anything that they don't do well because that was just, like, you know, a potential legal storm.
So, totally get what you mean.
Yeah, even for the stickers that we put out, we made sure anything and everything we write about competitors is just factual and there's no, you know, comparative language used per se.
Yeah.
Yeah, that's very, very common.
It's the safest way to go about it.
Okay, I think, yeah, and another pending item.
from us is we're gonna.
Yeah, and this with
We, Sydney and Marisol, have been doing additional keyword research, especially based on the feedback that you've provided so far.
So they're going to upload those assignments into Airtable as well, and then we're going to let you know when it's ready for you guys to review and approve.
All right, sounds good.
Okay, perfect.
Do you have anything else you want to talk about?
I think I'm good.

I do look forward to the meetings with devs, and maybe after that visibility to the whole project of CV databases, vulnerability databases, we can put forward a timeline on how far does the go-live date look like.
So it's a kind of priority.
I get pushed for it.
So I want to set the right expectations.
Let's just set some timelines for it now.
Okay, okay.
That's good to know.
We'll definitely, I'll mention this to the team.
and our stand-up so we can brainstorm something for you.
All right.
Sounds good.
All right.
Well, thank you so much.
I will see you later.
All right.
See you.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Discuss SentinelOne project updates, including CVE pages, content workflow, and upcoming dev team integration.](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=41.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [5 sample CVE pages created; AI-generated content (incl. pseudocode) awaiting security team review](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=44.0)
  - [ContentStack CMS integration meeting scheduled; Nicholas needs access for workflow mapping](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=363.0)
  - [2 new AI-generated blogs in progress; 1st blog revised (3000+ words) ready for review](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=626.0)
  - [Timeline for CVE/vulnerability database go-live needed; high priority for SentinelOne](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=1103.0)

## Topics

### CVE Page Samples and Content Workflow

  - [5 random CVE pages created using AI-powered content workflow](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=53.0)
  - [Structure consistent across all CVE pages](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=104.0)
  - [AI-generated pseudocode included with disclaimer](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=144.0)
  - [Previous code reviews for other clients found AI-generated code accurate](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)
  - [Ankit to share pages internally, including with security team for review](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=221.0)

### CMS Integration and Dev Team Meeting

  - [SentinelOne uses ContentStack (headless CMS), not Sanity as previously thought](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=505.0)
  - [Meeting scheduled with Nicholas, Jory, and web teams to discuss CMS access and integration](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=341.0)
  - [Nicholas needs CMS access to map workflow correctly](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=363.0)
  - [Integration help and bandwidth allocation to be discussed](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=427.0)

### AI Content Updates

  - [2 new blogs in progress, to be delivered by end of week](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=614.0)
  - [1st blog revised (3000+ words) ready for review and publishing](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=640.0)
  - [Mansi to handle next steps for content review](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=655.0)

### Keyword Volume Data in Airtable

  - [Data sourced from SEMrush](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=692.0)
  - [Search volumes may reflect multiple keywords for URL-based assignments](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=741.0)
  - [Consideration to simplify by using single keyword search volume in future](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=810.0)
  - [SentinelOne follows pillar and cluster strategy for content structuring](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=858.0)

### Content Strategy Adjustments

  - [Listicle articles paused due to internal discussions about competitor visibility](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=895.0)
  - [Some topics adjusted based on SEO analysis and keyword targeting](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=926.0)
  - [Legal considerations for mentioning competitors in content](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=1020.0)

### Additional Keyword Research

  - [Sydney and Marisol conducting further keyword research based on feedback](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=1078.0)
  - [New assignments to be uploaded to Airtable for review and approval](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=1088.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Ankit to review and share CVE page samples internally, including with security team](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=221.0)
  - [Mansi to review and handle next steps for revised AI-generated blog content](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=655.0)
  - [Nicholas to attend CMS integration meeting and gain necessary access](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=363.0)
  - [growthx.ai team to brainstorm and propose timeline for CVE/vulnerability database go-live](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=1103.0)
  - [Sydney and Marisol to upload new keyword research assignments to Airtable for review](https://fathom.video/share/yGFr5fDcMjJQjdxnx532SjZDtvzXerHB?tab=summary&timestamp=1088.0)

action_items.description: Review 5 CVE pages in Figma doc; share w/ Mahati & security team for feedback
Inform Nicholas re ContentStack CMS use; relay keyword volume feedback to Sydney
Review updated 3000+ word CS 101 blog post; decide on publishing
Discuss CVE database timeline w/ team post-dev meeting; prep for Ankit
